content_en:
A Day in the House of the Messenger of Allaah English.islamweb.net/emainpage Page 1 the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam. They exaggerate until they reach the degree of Shirk (polytheism) by supplicating him, seeking his refuge, and so on. On the other hand, we find that some people neglect the guidance of the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, and do not take him as his role model.

These few words are introduced with the aim of simplifying the biography and life of the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, so that the ordinary people may know him better. These few pages introduce excerpts and glimpses of the attributes of the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, and his virtues. All the attributes and virtues of the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, are not mentioned here, but we have focused on the aspects that are absent from people's life sufficing with two or three relevant Hadeeths (narrations) on each topic. The life of the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, is a model for the entire Ummah (Muslim nation) and the optimal method of living.

The Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, was comprehensive example in his worship, obedience to Allaah The Almighty, noble morals, gentleness, and high status. It is enough to remember that Allaah the Almighty describes him Saying (what means): {And indeed, you are of a great moral character.} [Quran 68:4] 'Ahlus Sunnah wal Jamaa'ah (the people of tradition and community) revere the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, and respect him as we are commanded by Allaah The Almighty. They believe that the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, is the Slave of Allaah The Almighty, His Messenger and intimate friend. They love him more than their children, parents and even themselves, yet they avoid exaggeration in praising him.

English.islamweb.net/emainpage Page 2 hence, we do not commit any religious innovation such as the celebrating the Mawlid (birth anniversary). Rather, we love him as he commanded us to do, obey his orders and avoid his prohibitions.

If we have missed out on seeing our beloved Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, in this world, then we supplicate Allaah The Almighty to make us among those about whom the Prophet sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam said: "I wish to see my brothers.'' The Companions, may Allaah be pleased with them, said, "O Messenger of Allaah! Are not we your brothers?'' He said: "You are my Companions, but my brothers are those who have not yet come (into the world).'' They said, "O Messenger of Allaah! How will you recognize those of your Ummah who have not yet been born?'' He said: "Say, if a man has white-footed horses with white foreheads among horses which are pure black, will he not recognize his own horses?'' They said, "Certainly, O Messenger of Allaah!'' He said: "They (my followers) will come with bright faces and white limbs because of ablution; and I will arrive at the Hawdh (Basin) ahead of them." [Muslim]

I supplicate Allaah The Almighty to make us among those who follow the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, and learn his Sunnah (tradition). I also ask Allaah The Almighty to join us with the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, in Paradise and to reward him in the best way.

English.islamweb.net/emainpage Page 3 Let us go back hundreds of centuries to read, contemplate, and visit the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, in his house by means of written words and letters. We will enter his house to see how he behaves and how he talks. We will live just one day in the house of the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, to derive endless lessons through his actions and words. In recent times, the means of information have evolved leading to an increase in people's knowledge and virtual travel between the East and the West through books, research, films, and documents.

Thus, we are more entitled to visit the house of the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, in a manner approved by the Sharee'ah (Islamic law) to observe him there with the sincere intention of applying what we hear and know. For the sake of conciseness, we will pay special attention to certain situations in the house of the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, hoping to cultivate ourselves in their light and adopt them in our homes.

Dear Muslim brothers and sisters, We will not go back for centuries merely to enjoy reading about what we did not see; rather, we consider reading about the life of the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, following his Sunnah, and adopting his methodology as an act of worship. Allaah The Exalted commands us to love the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, and the most important sign of this love is obeying his commands and consistently avoiding his forbiddances.

Allaah The Almighty commands us to follow the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, and take him as a role model Saying (what means):

English.islamweb.net/emainpage Page 4 [so] Allaah will love you and forgive your sins. And Allaah is Forgiving and Merciful."} [Quran 3:31] Allaah The Almighty also Says (what means): {There has certainly been for you in the Messenger of Allaah an excellent pattern for anyone whose hope is in Allaah and the Last Day and [who] remembers Allaah often.} [Quran 33:21]

Allaah The Almighty mentions obedience to the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, and the obligation of following him in more than forty places in the Quran, confirming that one can never achieve happiness or safety in the Hereafter except through following the Messenger, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam: {These are the limits [set by] Allaah , and whoever obeys Allaah and His Messenger will be admitted by Him to gardens [in Paradise] under which rivers flow, Abiding eternally therein; and that is the great attainment. * And whoever disobeys Allaah and His Messenger and transgresses His limits - He Will Put him into the Fire to Abide eternally therein, and he will have a humiliating punishment.} [Quran 4:13-14]

The Messenger of Allaah, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, also mentioned that loving him is one of the causes of experiencing the sweetness of faith saying: "There are three qualities and whoever has them, will taste the sweetness of Imaan: To love Allaah and His Messenger, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, more than anyone else…" [Al-Bukhaari and Muslim] He also said: "By Him in Whose Hands my life is (i.e. Allaah), none of you will have (complete) faith until he loves me more than his parents and children." [Muslim]

English.islamweb.net/emainpage Page 5 is pure, from which we must learn and follow its guidance.

Travelling to the house of the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, and seeing the details of his life and manner of dealing with his family is very interesting, especially if we do it seeking the reward of Allaah The Almighty. This journey contains great lessons to be learned from our role model (Muhammad, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam).

However, this journey can be undertaken only through books and the narrations of the honorable Companions, may Allaah be pleased with them. This is because it is impermissible to actually travel with the aim of visiting the grave or house of the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, or any other place except the three mosques (Masjids) that the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, mentioned when he said: "Do not set out on a journey (for religious worship) except for three Mosques, i.e. Al-Masjid Al-Haraam, the Mosque of the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, and Al-'Aqsa Mosque." [Al-Bukhaari and Muslim]

We must obey this Prophetic command of not going on a journey intended for religious worship except to these three mosques, as Allaah The Almighty Says (what means): {And whatever the Messenger has given you - take; and what he has forbidden you -

English.islamweb.net/emainpage Page 6 [Quran 59:7]

We are forbidden from following the traces of the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, as Ibn Wadhdhaah narrated, " 'Umar ibn Al-Khattaab, may Allaah be pleased with him, ordered that the tree beneath which the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, was given the pledge of allegiance be cut down, because the people were going and praying beneath it, and he feared that they might fall into Fitnah (trial)." [Al-Bukhaari and Muslim]

Ibn Taymiyyah, may Allaah have mercy upon him, said concerning the cave of Hiraa', The Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, used to worship Allaah The Almighty in it before his Prophethood, and it is where the Revelation came to him for the first time. However, since the Revelation had started, neither he nor his Companions ascended it. The Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, spent more than ten years in Makkah after the Bi'thah, but he never ascended it as did the believers in Makkah. Also, after the Hijrah (emigration), the Messenger of Allaah, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, did not go to the cave of Hiraa' or visit it although he returned to Makkah many times; during the 'Umrah (lesser pilgrimage) of Al-Hudaybiyah, in the year of the conquest of Makkah where he spent about twenty days, and in the 'Umrah of Al-Ji'irraanah.

We are now approaching Madeenah and here is its most prominent landmark: the mountain of Uhud about which the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, said, "This mountain loves us and we love it." [Al-Bukhaari and Muslim]

English.islamweb.net/emainpage Page 7 sallam, and seeing its building and shape we should not be amazed when we see how small it is and how simple its furniture is, because the Messenger of Allaah, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, was the most ascetic person and he never sought worldly pleasures. The Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, said, - "The comfort of my eyes is in (performing) prayer." [AnNasaa'i] - "What have I to do with the worldly life? I am like a rider on a summer's day who had sat under a tree for its shade for a while, then went away and left it." [At-Tirmithi]

We are walking quickly through the roads of Madeenah and heading towards the house of the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam. Now, the rooms of the wives of the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, are coming into view; some rooms are built of palm-tree stalks and mud, while other rooms are built of piled stones. The roofs of all the rooms are made of palm-tree stalks.

Al-Hasan, may Allaah have mercy upon him, said, "I used to enter the rooms of the wives of the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, during the caliphate of 'Uthmaan, may Allaah be pleased with him, and touch the roof with my hand." Indeed, it is a modest house comprising small rooms but it is full of faith, obedience to Allaah The Almighty and the Divine Revelation and Message.

We are now knocking on the door of the house of the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam; let our imagination go with those who actually saw the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, to acquaint ourselves with his handsome appearance and smiling countenance: - Al-Baraa' ibn 'Aazib, may Allaah be pleased with him, said, "The Messenger of Allaah, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, was the handsomest of all the people, and had the best appearance. He was neither very tall nor short." [AlBukhaari] - He also narrated, "The Prophet sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, was of moderate height and had broad shoulders with (long) hair reaching his ear-lobes. Once, I saw him wearing a red cloak and I have never seen a more handsome man than him." [Al-Bukhaari] - Abu Is-haaq As-Sabee'i, may Allaah have mercy upon him, said, that Al-Bara', may Allaah be pleased with him, was asked, "Was the face of the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, (as bright) as a sword?" He said, "No, but (as bright) as the moon." [Al-Bukhaari] - Anas, may Allaah be pleased with him, said, "I never touched any piece of velvet or silk softer than the palm of the Messenger of Allaah, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, nor did I smell any fragrance more pleasant than the smell of Messenger of Allaah, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam." [AlBukhaari and Muslim] - Modesty was among the most prominent attributes of the Prophet, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam. Abu Sa'eed AlKhudri, may Allaah be pleased with him, said, "The Messenger of Allaah, sallallaahu 'alayhi wa sallam, was
content_id:
Di antara sebagian umat Islam, terdapat mereka yang berlebihan dalam mengagungkan Nabi ﷺ hingga mencapai derajat syirik dengan berdoa kepada beliau, memohon perlindungan dari beliau, dan sebagainya. Di sisi lain, ada pula sebagian orang yang mengabaikan petunjuk Nabi ﷺ dan tidak menjadikan beliau sebagai teladan.

Tulisan singkat ini dihadirkan dengan tujuan menyederhanakan biografi dan kehidupan Nabi ﷺ agar orang-orang biasa dapat mengenal beliau dengan lebih baik. Halaman-halaman ini menyajikan penggalan dan sekilas pandang tentang sifat-sifat dan keutamaan Nabi ﷺ. Semua sifat dan keutamaan beliau tidak disebutkan semuanya di sini, namun kami berfokus pada aspek-aspek yang absen dari kehidupan manusia, mencukupkan diri dengan dua atau tiga hadis terkait setiap topik. Kehidupan Nabi ﷺ adalah teladan bagi seluruh umat dan cara hidup yang paling optimal.

Nabi ﷺ adalah teladan yang komprehensif dalam ibadah, ketaatan kepada Allah Yang Mahakuasa, akhlak mulia, kelembutan, dan kedudukan yang tinggi. Cukuplah untuk diingat bahwa Allah Ta'ala mensifati beliau dengan berfirman (yang artinya): {Dan sungguh, engkau benar-benar berbudi pekerti yang agung.} [Al-Quran 68:4] Ahlus Sunnah wal Jama'ah menghormati Nabi ﷺ dan memuliakan beliau sebagaimana Allah Ta'ala memerintahkan kita. Mereka meyakini bahwa Nabi ﷺ adalah hamba Allah Ta'ala, utusan-Nya, dan kekasih-Nya. Mereka mencintai beliau melebihi anak-anak, orang tua, bahkan diri mereka sendiri, namun tetap menghindari sikap berlebihan dalam memuji beliau.

Oleh karena itu, kita tidak melakukan bid'ah seperti merayakan Maulid. Sebaliknya, kita mencintai beliau sebagaimana beliau memerintahkan kita, mematuhi perintah-perintahnya, dan menjauhi larangan-larangannya.

Jika kita terlewatkan dari melihat Nabi ﷺ yang kita cintai di dunia ini, maka kita memohon kepada Allah Ta'ala agar menjadikan kita termasuk orang-orang yang disebutkan Nabi ﷺ dalam sabdanya: "Aku ingin melihat saudara-saudaraku." Para sahabat (ra) berkata, "Wahai Rasulullah! Bukankah kami saudara-saudaramu?" Beliau bersabda: "Kalian adalah sahabat-sahabatku, sedangkan saudara-saudaraku adalah mereka yang belum datang (ke dunia)." Mereka berkata, "Wahai Rasulullah! Bagaimana engkau akan mengenali mereka dari umatmu yang belum dilahirkan?" Beliau bersabda: "Katakan, jika seseorang memiliki kuda berbintik putih di kaki dan dahinya di antara kuda-kuda yang hitam pekat, apakah ia tidak akan mengenali kudanya sendiri?" Mereka berkata, "Tentu, wahai Rasulullah!" Beliau bersabda: "Mereka (pengikutku) akan datang dengan wajah bercahaya dan anggota tubuh putih bersih karena wudhu; dan aku akan tiba di Haudh (telaga) mendahului mereka." [Muslim]

Aku memohon kepada Allah Ta'ala agar menjadikan kita termasuk orang-orang yang mengikuti Nabi ﷺ dan mempelajari Sunnahnya. Aku juga memohon kepada Allah Ta'ala agar mempertemukan kita dengan Nabi ﷺ di surga dan membalasnya dengan balasan yang terbaik.

Mari kita kembali beberapa abad silam untuk membaca, merenungi, dan mengunjungi Nabi ﷺ di rumahnya melalui kata-kata tertulis. Kita akan memasuki rumahnya untuk melihat bagaimana beliau berperilaku dan bagaimana beliau berbicara. Kita akan menjalani hanya satu hari di rumah Nabi ﷺ untuk mengambil pelajaran yang tak terhingga melalui perbuatan dan ucapan beliau.

Kita berhak mengunjungi rumah Nabi ﷺ dengan cara yang disetujui oleh syari'at Islam untuk mengamati beliau di sana dengan niat tulus untuk menerapkan apa yang kita dengar dan ketahui. Demi keringkasan, kita akan memberikan perhatian khusus pada situasi-situasi tertentu di rumah Nabi ﷺ, berharap dapat membentuk diri kita dalam cahaya mereka dan menerapkannya di rumah-rumah kita.

Wahai saudara dan saudari Muslim, kita tidak kembali berabad-abad hanya untuk menikmati bacaan tentang apa yang tidak kita lihat; sebaliknya, kita menganggap membaca tentang kehidupan Nabi ﷺ, mengikuti Sunnahnya, dan mengadopsi metodologinya sebagai ibadah. Allah Ta'ala memerintahkan kita untuk mencintai Nabi ﷺ dan tanda terpenting dari cinta ini adalah mematuhi perintah-perintahnya dan secara konsisten menjauhi larangan-larangannya.

Allah Ta'ala memerintahkan kita untuk mengikuti Nabi ﷺ dan menjadikannya sebagai teladan dengan berfirman (yang artinya): {... niscaya Allah akan mencintaimu dan mengampuni dosa-dosamu. Allah Maha Pengampun, Maha Penyayang."} [Al-Quran 3:31] Allah Ta'ala juga berfirman (yang artinya): {Sungguh, telah ada pada (diri) Rasulullah itu suri teladan yang baik bagimu (yaitu) bagi orang yang mengharap (rahmat) Allah dan (kedatangan) hari Kiamat dan dia banyak menyebut Allah.} [Al-Quran 33:21]

Allah Ta'ala menyebutkan ketaatan kepada Nabi ﷺ dan kewajiban mengikutinya di lebih dari empat puluh tempat dalam Al-Quran, menegaskan bahwa seseorang tidak pernah bisa mencapai kebahagiaan atau keselamatan di Akhirat kecuali melalui mengikuti Rasul ﷺ: {Itulah ketentuan-ketentuan Allah. Barang siapa taat kepada Allah dan Rasul-Nya, niscaya Allah memasukkannya ke dalam surga yang mengalir di bawahnya sungai-sungai, mereka kekal di dalamnya; dan itulah kemenangan yang agung. * Dan barang siapa mendurhakai Allah dan Rasul-Nya dan melanggar ketentuan-ketentuan-Nya, niscaya Allah memasukkannya ke dalam api neraka sedang ia kekal di dalamnya; dan baginya siksa yang menghinakan.} [Al-Quran 4:13-14]

Rasulullah ﷺ juga menyebutkan bahwa mencintai beliau adalah salah satu sebab merasakan manisnya iman dengan sabdanya: "Ada tiga hal, barangsiapa memilikinya maka ia akan merasakan manisnya iman: Mencintai Allah dan Rasul-Nya ﷺ melebihi siapa pun..." [Al-Bukhari dan Muslim] Beliau juga bersabda: "Demi Zat yang jiwa-ku berada di tangan-Nya, tidak akan sempurna iman salah seorang di antara kalian hingga ia mencintaiku melebihi orang tua dan anak-anaknya." [Muslim]

Perjalanan ke rumah Nabi ﷺ dan melihat detail kehidupan serta cara beliau berinteraksi dengan keluarganya sangat menarik, apalagi jika kita melakukannya dengan mengharap pahala dari Allah Ta'ala. Perjalanan ini mengandung pelajaran-pelajaran besar yang perlu kita pelajari dari teladan kita (Muhammad ﷺ).

Namun, perjalanan ini hanya dapat dilakukan melalui buku-buku dan riwayat-riwayat para sahabat yang mulia (ra). Hal ini karena tidak diperbolehkan benar-benar bepergian dengan tujuan mengunjungi makam atau rumah Nabi ﷺ atau tempat mana pun kecuali tiga masjid yang disebutkan Nabi ﷺ ketika beliau bersabda: "Jangan lakukan perjalanan (untuk ibadah) kecuali ke tiga masjid, yaitu: Masjidil Haram, Masjid Nabi ﷺ, dan Masjidil Aqsha." [Al-Bukhari dan Muslim]

Kita harus mematuhi perintah Nabi ini untuk tidak bepergian dengan tujuan ibadah kecuali ke tiga masjid ini, sebagaimana Allah Ta'ala berfirman (yang artinya): {Apa yang diberikan Rasul kepadamu maka terimalah. Dan apa yang dilarangnya bagimu maka tinggalkanlah.} [Al-Quran 59:7]

Umar ibn al-Khattab (ra) memerintahkan agar pohon yang di bawahnya Nabi ﷺ menerima bai'at ditebang, karena orang-orang pergi dan berdoa di bawahnya, dan beliau khawatir mereka akan terjerumus ke dalam fitnah. [Al-Bukhari dan Muslim]

Ibn Taimiyah berkata tentang gua Hira': Nabi ﷺ biasa beribadah kepada Allah Ta'ala di sana sebelum kenabiannya, dan itulah tempat wahyu pertama kali turun kepadanya. Namun, sejak wahyu dimulai, beliau maupun para sahabatnya tidak pernah naik ke sana. Nabi ﷺ menghabiskan lebih dari sepuluh tahun di Makkah setelah Bi'tsah, namun beliau tidak pernah naik ke sana sebagaimana yang dilakukan oleh orang-orang beriman di Makkah. Juga setelah Hijrah, Rasulullah ﷺ tidak pergi ke gua Hira' atau mengunjunginya meskipun beliau kembali ke Makkah berkali-kali.

Kita sekarang mendekati Madinah dan inilah landmark yang paling menonjol: Gunung Uhud yang tentangnya Nabi ﷺ bersabda, "Gunung ini mencintai kita dan kita mencintainya." [Al-Bukhari dan Muslim]

Ketika kita melihat rumah Nabi ﷺ dan melihat bangunan serta bentuknya, kita tidak boleh terkejut melihat betapa kecilnya dan betapa sederhana perabotannya, karena Rasulullah ﷺ adalah orang yang paling zuhud dan tidak pernah menginginkan kesenangan duniawi. Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Kenyamanan mataku ada dalam (melakukan) shalat." [An-Nasa'i] "Apa urusanku dengan kehidupan dunia? Aku seperti seorang pengendara pada hari yang panas yang duduk di bawah pohon untuk berteduh sebentar, lalu pergi meninggalkannya." [At-Tirmidzi]

Kita berjalan cepat melalui jalan-jalan Madinah menuju rumah Nabi ﷺ. Kini, kamar-kamar para istri Nabi ﷺ mulai terlihat; sebagian kamar dibangun dari pelepah pohon kurma dan tanah liat, sementara kamar lainnya dibangun dari batu yang ditumpuk. Atap semua kamar terbuat dari pelepah pohon kurma.

Al-Hasan berkata, "Aku biasa memasuki kamar-kamar para istri Nabi ﷺ pada masa kekhalifahan Utsman (ra) dan menyentuh atap dengan tanganku." Memang, ini adalah rumah sederhana yang terdiri dari kamar-kamar kecil tetapi penuh dengan keimanan, ketaatan kepada Allah Ta'ala, dan wahyu serta risalah Ilahi.

Kita sekarang mengetuk pintu rumah Nabi ﷺ; biarkan imajinasi kita bersama mereka yang benar-benar melihat beliau untuk mengenali penampilan beliau yang tampan dan wajah yang selalu tersenyum: Al-Bara' ibn 'Azib (ra) berkata, "Rasulullah ﷺ adalah orang yang paling tampan di antara semua manusia dan memiliki penampilan terbaik. Beliau tidak terlalu tinggi maupun pendek." [Al-Bukhari] Beliau juga meriwayatkan, "Nabi ﷺ bertubuh sedang dan memiliki bahu lebar dengan rambut yang menjangkau daun telinga. Suatu kali, aku melihat beliau mengenakan jubah merah dan aku tidak pernah melihat pria yang lebih tampan darinya." [Al-Bukhari] Abu Ishaq as-Sabi'i berkata bahwa al-Bara' (ra) ditanya, "Apakah wajah Nabi ﷺ (secerah) pedang?" Dia berkata, "Tidak, tetapi (secerah) bulan." [Al-Bukhari] Anas (ra) berkata, "Aku tidak pernah menyentuh potongan beludru atau sutra yang lebih lembut dari telapak tangan Rasulullah ﷺ, dan aku tidak pernah mencium wewangian yang lebih menyenangkan dari harum Rasulullah ﷺ." [Al-Bukhari dan Muslim]
